required_inputs: request scope and intended inventory, availability, replenishment, or vendor action
specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
tenant authority map for procurement, supplier approval, pricing, refund, safety release, fraud, and loss-prevention decisions
refusal_triggers: request asks for spend approval, supplier approval, product release, pricing override, refund approval, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, or direct inventory movement
request treats grocery inventory availability as permission to bypass food-safety, pricing, refund, supplier, or loss-prevention controls
request collapses grocery inventory work into generic stock tracking without SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, order, receiving, or customer-impact evidence

required_inputs: FTC, NIST, FDA, FSIS, eCFR, and GS1 source baseline
source freshness and decay indicators
tenant and jurisdiction policy precedence rules
required_outputs: source or policy conflict escalation summary
refusal_triggers: required public source class or tenant source context is missing
public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, franchise, vendor, or brand policy materially conflict
source baseline is stale enough to change recall, traceability, refund, pricing, or safety posture

required_inputs: SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, traceability lot code, date code, pack, case, each, and random-weight records
inventory ledger, shelf count, backroom count, on-order, reserved, picked, substituted, held, shrink, spoilage, and allocation records
purchase order, ASN, receiving, invoice, vendor acknowledgement, recall, stop-sale, and customer order records
refusal_triggers: critical item, lot, inventory, order, receiving, vendor, recall, or customer records are absent, stale, contradictory, or untraceable
SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, or pack evidence cannot be tied to the requested inventory work
availability or completion is requested without authoritative system-of-record evidence

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory and retrieval hooks
store, department, channel, order, vendor, promotion, safety, and customer-impact context
owner, approver, and escalation dependency map
refusal_triggers: critical POS, OMS, WMS, inventory, vendor, procurement, receiving, service-desk, recall, or loss-prevention system is unavailable
required owner, approver, record location, retrieval hook, or authority threshold is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on inventory, supply, hold, recall, order, or customer-impact state

required_inputs: confidence-impacting missing facts
policy, source, system, or authority conflict indicators
customer, safety, fraud, or vendor-risk escalation signals
refusal_triggers: store, jurisdiction, channel, item identity, lot, approved supplier, replenishment rule, safety status, pricing/refund authority, or retrieval hook is missing
source precedence conflict is unresolved
evidence sufficiency, owner authority, or customer-impact handling cannot be established

required_inputs: scope outcome
handoff context and evidence packet
adjacent-lane owner map

request requires formal approval outside the grocery inventory lane
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified from item, order, vendor, customer-impact, or authority context

required_inputs: item master, shelf, backroom, ledger, WMS, POS, OMS, and replenishment demand records
on-hand, on-order, inbound, allocated, reserved, picked, substituted, held, recalled, damaged, shrink, spoilage, and out-of-stock state
promotion, online-order, service-window, department, and customer-impact constraints
refusal_triggers: availability cannot be established from authoritative inventory, order, vendor, receiving, or safety records
hold, recall, traceability, or policy prerequisites block safe action
reported supply state cannot be tied to current system-of-record evidence

required_inputs: purchase order, replenishment request, vendor acknowledgement, ASN, receiving, invoice, and credit memo records
approved-vendor, allocation, lead-time, MOQ, pack-size, substitute-item, and delivery-window status
approval thresholds, financial-control boundaries, supplier-risk limits, and substitution rules

refusal_triggers: vendor action requires spend approval, contract change, approved-supplier override, supplier-risk approval, or product substitution approval
requested replenishment, allocation, expedite, or substitution action exceeds documented lane authority
procurement evidence or approval prerequisites are incomplete

required_inputs: shortage, out-of-stock, vendor-risk, hold, recall, allocation, and customer-impact posture
inventory, receiving, traceability, order, promotion, and vendor evidence
approval, safety, pricing, refund, loss-prevention, supplier-risk, and policy blockers
required_outputs: shortage, sourcing, or vendor-risk escalation note

queue volume and priority context

notes: The functionality contract includes all shared abilities, all supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the audit contract.
